doctrine: Learn from others' mistakes more than our own. Every entry converts an OBSERVED external failure into a law and a guard BEFORE it can bite us. Own-incident entries are the exception that prove the cost of learning first-hand.

entries.mistake: Stale SEO titles: 'Best Hybrid Strains of 2025' served in July 2026; homepage claims 39 DC dispensaries while the listing page returns 48.
entries.law: Every displayed count and dated claim must be query-derived at render time; nothing user-visible is hardcoded to a year or a count.

guard.reference: All directory counts render from prisma counts; freshness labels are computed from verifiedAt at request time (src/lib/freshness.mjs).

entries.mistake: Revenue-critical merchant signup (/add-business) is a hard 404; their supply-side funnel has been broken in public.
entries.law: Revenue-critical funnels get synthetic end-to-end checks; a broken funnel is a release-gate failure, not a support ticket.

guard.reference: http-public-write-check.mjs + http-business-check.mjs exercise claim/correction/login flows every gate run.

entries.mistake: Anti-bot wall returns 406 on ALL listing/deal pages to every non-browser fetcher — making their core data invisible to AI answer engines while they simultaneously publish an llm.txt inviting AI in.
entries.evidence: Live curl matrix, field recon 2026-07-23; llm.txt exists (10.3KB) yet its linked pages 406.
entries.law: AI-visibility must be verified END-TO-END (fetchability of the actual data), never assumed from the presence of policy files. Never deploy bot protection that breaks machine-readability of public data.

guard.reference: test:http fetches public pages with raw HTTP (no cookies/JS) and asserts 200 + content; llms.txt served and asserted.

entries.mistake: Blanket GPTBot block with 26 hand-picked affiliate re-allows — surrendering AI-answer-engine presence for core data while feeding only commission content.
entries.evidence: robots.txt fetched 2026-07-23; shadow baseline hash recorded.
entries.law: Crawler policy is strategy and gets re-derived each SENSE cycle from measured referral value; answer-engine bots that cite us stay allowed, training-only bots stay blocked.

guard.reference: robots.ts four-bot split + guard test; shadow engine hashes competitor robots for policy-shift detection.

entries.mistake: 5.0-star ratings displayed at full visual weight from samples of 1-22 reviews under the headline 'authentic reviews' — statistically hollow social proof.
entries.evidence: DC listing screenshots 2026-07-23; Holistic District 5.0 from 1 review.
entries.law: No unverifiable or statistically hollow social proof, ever. Display nothing rather than a misleading number.

guard.reference: Truth law: no synthetic ratings; structured-data tests assert aggregateRating is never emitted for our records.

entries.mistake: Maryland/Virginia businesses bleed into 'Washington DC' surfaces (109 claimed DC dispensaries, ~10-15 真 DC) — geographic dilution that erodes local trust.
entries.evidence: Field recon 2026-07-23: Trulieve Halethorpe 31mi, Greenwave 50mi in DC results.
entries.law: Geographic integrity is absolute: the DC universe is ABCA-registry-derived; no out-of-district records ever enter DC surfaces.

guard.reference: Universe seeded exclusively from DC ABCA registry (seed-abca-retailers.mjs); tenant isolation + bounded-search checks.

entries.mistake: High-intent consumer URL /strains redirects visitors to a B2B advertising pitch — sacrificing user trust and organic intent for a sales funnel.
entries.evidence: Field recon 2026-07-23: leafly.com/strains -> 'Retail Solutions For Your Dispensary'.
entries.law: Consumer-intent URLs are never repurposed for B2B capture; canonical surfaces keep their promise.

guard.reference: Canonical literals guard-tested per route (selfCanonicalPublicRoutes in http-truth-check); /strains serves strain content.

entries.mistake: Deal surfaces without expiry dates or verification (Leafly), zero indexed DC deal pages (Weedmaps), 404 deals hub + '0 deals claimed' on top merchants (Where's Weed) — stale-deal ambiguity everywhere.
entries.law: Time-bounded claims must display their validity window and machine-readable validThrough; expired-but-active deals are a failing health check.

guard.reference: Deal.expiryDate required in schema; deals page shows expiry + freshness; dealItemListJsonLd emits validThrough; sitemind deal-freshness check FAILs on expired-active.

entries.mistake: Migrated DC URLs to a new hierarchy and let ALL legacy short-form city URLs 404 — orphaning years of backlinks and search equity.
entries.law: URL migrations always ship 301/308 maps; no public URL is ever abandoned to 404.

guard.reference: www→apex 308 path-preserving redirect + REDIRECT_MAP pattern in proxy.ts; any future path change must extend it (guard-tested).

entries.mistake: Editorial strain copy makes unlabeled medical claims ('patients often use Blue Dream to treat...') with only a site-footer FDA disclaimer — regulatory exposure baked into content.
entries.law: No medical claims in any copy, editorial or merchant-supplied; effect language is labeled as marketing convention with inline disclaimers.

guard.reference: Strain pages carry inline not-medical-advice captions; ad-creative verification scans FORBIDDEN_CLAIMS; guard tests forbid truth-claim regressions.

entries.mistake: SPAC-fueled expansion ahead of unit economics: public listing collapsed ~99% and the company deregistered — growth spending outran durable revenue.
entries.law: Sequencing law: no paid-placement auctions, payment rails, or expansion spend before $20K MRR of proven merchant revenue; external-spend-zero until then.

guard.reference: Sequencing + external-spend-zero laws in sentinel doctrine; EARN cycle reports against them.

entries.mistake: Opaque enterprise pricing (~$2,805/mo average) with sales-gated onboarding in an SMB market — paying-client counts declined while small operators churned or never signed.
entries.evidence: docs/competitive/corporate-history.md; business-surface recon 2026-07-23 (no public pricing, contact-sales).
entries.law: Small-merchant markets demand published pricing and same-day self-serve; the sales-call wall is a competitor gift we never build.

guard.reference: /pricing published rate card; self-serve claim flow; outreach doctrine quotes their walls.

entries.mistake: Years of unlicensed-seller listings created regulatory blowback and a forced purge (the 'Report an Unlicensed CA Seller' footer link is the scar tissue).
entries.law: Verification-first data model: unverified records are labeled AWAITING_VERIFICATION and never presented as verified; platform never vouches for what it has not checked.

guard.reference: dataStatus chain + isPubliclyVerified gates every public claim, JSON-LD, and ad; guard tests forbid self-certification.

entries.actor: orderweeddc (the exception that proves the rule)
entries.mistake: Turbopack standalone artifact shipped with an unresolvable hashed Prisma external; a repo-contaminated smoke test masked it. Cost: ~2 hours of founder terminal time and 4 failed production attempts.
entries.law: OWD-D1..D8 (isolation-first artifact proof, hypothesis!=proof, DB-hash guard, live GitHub state, command-path discipline).

guard.reference: Webpack-only builder + unresolved-external scan + true-isolation runtime test + verify-and-deploy.sh + deployment-integrity tests.
